popularStory.headline: Memorial
popularStory.description: HENRY de Hastings, of Ashil, Norfolk, son of WILLIAM de Hastings & his first wife Margery Bigod of Norfolk (-before 9 Aug 1250). King Henry III granted "custodia terre et heredis Willelmi de Hasting" …

burialPlace.address: Astbury, Cheshire East Unitary Authority, Cheshire, England

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
«b»Birth «/b»
Henry was approved to inherit his father's possessions soon after his father's death was announced. He must therefore have been at least 21 in January 1226.

«b»Father «/b»
Sir William de Hastings, Steward of the Royal Household & of the Abbey of Bury St. Edmunds, as shown by the Fine Rolls.

«b»Mother «/b»
Apparently Margaret le Bigod, as stated by Dugdale in his Baronage and Warwickshire. See father's article.

Sir Henry de Hastings, King's Steward of the Abbey of Bury St. Edmunds was born about 1205. Not including his wife's lands, he had Barwell, Burbach, Nailstone, & Wistow, Leicestershire. He also held Ashill in Norfolk, and more lands in Suffolk, Warwickshire, and other counties.

He married Ada of Huntingdon, daughter of David, 9th Earl of Huntingdon and Maud of Chester, between 1224 and 7 June 1237; They had 1 son (Sir Henry) and 3 daughters (Hillary, wife of Sir William de Harcourt, & of Robert de Frankeville; Ada, wife of Sir Hubert Hovel; & Margery). This later gave his descendants a claim on the Scottish throne.

Family with wife Ada of Huntingdon (d. abt 4 Aug 1241-30 Jun 1242):

1.) Sir Henry de Hastings, King's Steward of the Abbey of Bury St. Edmunds died circa 9 August 1250.

2.) Hillary, wife of Sir William de Harcourt, & of Robert de Frankeville

3.) Ada, wife of Sir Hubert Hovel

4.) Margery

He is said to have died before 9 Aug 1250, for example in Complete Peerage, apparently based on the Close Rolls.

No place of death seems to be mentioned in any such record. He could have died in France because Mathew Paris (Chronica Majora) mentions him going over to France in 1250, with a big party led by Richard Earl of Gloucester, going to see the Pope in Lyon, apparently to buy a priory in Deerhurst. Other sources say he was among the nobles who committed to crusade that year. Findagrave strangely says he had a monument in Cheshire.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Henry de Hastings於1196年出生在英國諾福爾可時，他的父親William de Hastings31歲，母親Margaret le Bigod14歲。他於1237年6月7日之前與Ada Huntingdon結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他於1242年登記服兵役。他去世於1250年8月9日在英國享年54歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭爾阿斯特伯里。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hastings
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from Hastings (Sussex), near which the English army was defeated by the Normans in 1066. The placename derives from an Old English personal name Hǣsta + the Old English groupname suffix -ingas. In the latter part of the 12th century, at the time of William the Lion, the surname was taken to Scotland, where it assimilated instances of the native Scottish surname Harestane.
English: variant of Hasting with Middle English genitival -s.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hOistín ‘descendant of Oistín’, the Gaelic form of the personal name Augustine (see Austin ).

variants.name: Hasting
meanings: English: from the Norman personal name Hastang, Hastenc, Hasten (Old Norse Hásteinn ‘high’ + ‘stone’). See also Hastings .

variants.name: Heston
meanings: English: habitational name from Heston (Middlesex) or Histon (Cambridgeshire). The Middlesex placename derives from Old English hǣs ‘brushwood’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, while the Cambridgeshire placename may derive from Old English hyse ‘shoot, tendril’, also ‘son, youth’, + tūn.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Henry de Hastings was born in 1196, in Norfolk, England, his father, William de Hastings, was 31 and his mother, Margaret le Bigod, was 14. He married Ada Huntingdon before 7 June 1237. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. He registered for military service in 1242. He died on 9 August 1250, in England, at the age of 54, and was buried in Astbury, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Henry de Hastings est né en 1196, à Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, William de Hastings, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Margaret le Bigod, avait 14 ans. Il a épousé Ada Huntingdon avant 7 juin 1237. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1242. Il est décédé le 9 août 1250, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterré à Astbury, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Juni 1237 Ada Huntingdon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er hat sich 1242 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb am 9. August 1250 im Alter von 54 Jahren in England und wurde in Astbury, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lui sposato Ada Huntingdon prima del 7 giugno 1237. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1242. Morì il 9 agosto 1250, nel England, all'età di 54 anni, e fu sepolto a Astbury, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Henry de Hastingsが1196年にイギリスノーフォルクで生まれた時，父William de Hastingsは31歳，母Margaret le Bigodは14歳であった。1237年6月7日以前にAda Huntingdonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1242年に兵役登録をした。彼は1250年8月9日にイギリスで54歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスチェシュアイアアストブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Henry de Hastings(이)가 1196년 영국 노르폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Hastings(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret le Bigod(은)는 14세였다. 그는 1237년 6월 7일 이전 Ada Huntingdon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1242년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1250년 8월 9일 영국에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 애스트버이에 묻혔다.

pt.Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, England,: Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Henry de Hastings nasceu em 1196, em Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, William de Hastings, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Margaret le Bigod, tinha 14 anos. Ele casou-se com Ada Huntingdon antes 7 de junho de 1237. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1242. Ele faleceu em 9 de agosto de 1250, em Inglaterra, com 54 anos, e foi sepultado em Astbury, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ro.Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, England,: Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, Anglia, United Kingdom

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Henry de Hastings родился в 1196 г. в местности норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени William de Hastings было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Margaret le Bigod, 14. Он женился на женщине по имени Ada Huntingdon перед 7 июнь 1237 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. В 1242 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 9 августа 1250 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 54 лет и был похоронен в местности астбури, чешир, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Ada Huntingdon antes de 7 de junio de 1237. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1242. Murió el 9 de agosto de 1250, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 54 años, y fue sepultado en Astbury, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
